findings.title: Cleanup execution flow is not implemented
findings.details: The app can analyze and present categories, but the main CTA only updates status text and there is no service API for delete, compress, merge, or apply-review actions.
findings.resolution_note: Implemented cleanup review and execution flow with explicit delete confirmation, live photo-library deletion service wiring, and dashboard refresh after execution.

history.note: Implemented cleanup review and execution flow with explicit delete confirmation, live photo-library deletion service wiring, and dashboard refresh after execution.

findings.title: Billing flow is scaffolded and lacks restore
findings.details: The live environment uses MockSubscriptionService, the paywall hardcodes prices, and there is no restore or manage-subscription flow.
findings.resolution_note: Replaced the live mock billing path with StoreKit-backed subscription loading, purchase, restore, and manage-subscription paywall wiring.

history.note: Replaced the live mock billing path with StoreKit-backed subscription loading, purchase, restore, and manage-subscription paywall wiring.

findings.title: Vault is not production-safe yet
findings.details: Vault files are stored under temporaryDirectory, the record index is in memory only, and the UI writes a sample record with the hardcoded passcode 1234.
findings.resolution_note: Moved vault storage to durable app-support storage, persisted the record index, removed the hardcoded passcode path, and added keychain-backed setup with optional biometric unlock.

history.note: Moved vault storage to durable app-support storage, persisted the record index, removed the hardcoded passcode path, and added keychain-backed setup with optional biometric unlock.

findings.title: Duplicate detection is disabled on live device assets
findings.details: The live asset mapper always sets checksum to nil, while duplicate detection groups only assets with non-nil checksums.

findings.title: Reclaimable space is double-counted
findings.details: The dashboard adds compression savings on top of deletion reclaimable bytes even though the compression estimate is derived from the same selected assets.
findings.resolution_note: Resolved by separating delete reclaimable bytes from compression estimates in the dashboard summary path; the dashboard no longer adds compression savings on top of deletion bytes.

history.note: Resolved by separating delete reclaimable bytes from compression estimates in the dashboard summary path; the dashboard no longer adds compression savings on top of deletion bytes.

findings.title: Breach-check dataset is demo-sized
findings.details: The local breach index bundled in the app has only three hashes, so the feature is not production-credible yet.

findings.title: Release validation coverage is too thin
findings.details: The repo has only four unit tests and no iOS UI, device, purchase, or permission-flow coverage.

findings.title: Heavy scan work is main-actor bound
findings.details: The service protocols are MainActor-isolated and the live photo scan enumerates the full library on that path, which risks UI stalls on large libraries.

findings.title: Configured vault was treated as writable after relaunch
findings.details: A configured vault reloaded as configured but locked after relaunch, while the dashboard save action still attempted to write immediately. Because the root key only lived in memory, post-relaunch writes failed and the recovery copy suggested reconfiguration instead of unlock.
findings.resolution_note: Implemented explicit vault access state plus unlock flows, added a vault unlock service entry point for configured-but-locked vaults, and covered relaunch/save/unlock regressions with tests.

history.note: Implemented explicit vault access state plus unlock flows, added a vault unlock service entry point for configured-but-locked vaults, and covered relaunch/save/unlock regressions with tests.

findings.title: Subscribed users were sent back to the paywall on scan failure
findings.details: After purchase or restore, a live-scan failure reused the paywall return stage. Active subscribers could land back on the purchase screen instead of retrying permissions or scan from a subscribed state.
findings.resolution_note: Changed subscribed scan-failure recovery to return to the permissions stage with active subscription state preserved, and added purchase/restore scan-failure regression tests.

history.note: Changed subscribed scan-failure recovery to return to the permissions stage with active subscription state preserved, and added purchase/restore scan-failure regression tests.

findings.title: Cleanup success was overwritten by post-delete refresh failure
findings.details: Cleanup execution could succeed, but any error while reloading the dashboard afterward changed the phase to failed and showed a generic failure message. Deleting the last remaining assets was a concrete trigger because the refresh could return an empty-library error.
findings.resolution_note: Separated cleanup execution success from post-delete refresh success, preserved completed cleanup results on refresh errors including empty-library cases, and added regression tests.

history.note: Separated cleanup execution success from post-delete refresh success, preserved completed cleanup results on refresh errors including empty-library cases, and added regression tests.

findings.title: App Store release is blocked by missing Apple Developer account
findings.details: The project currently does not have an Apple Developer account because of budget constraints. Continue implementing and hardening the app toward App Store readiness, but treat account-dependent release tasks such as App Store Connect setup, StoreKit product configuration, signing/distribution, and final submission as blocked until the account is available.

history.note: Keep building toward production quality and App Store compliance while deferring account-gated release steps.
